---
title: "ReBioClim analytical workflow collaboration notebook"
format: html
---


# prepare stream geometry (without segments)
- all osm waterway
    - from [`osm_stream_extract.py`](scripts/osm_stream_extract.py), osm way+relation, both extracted, for all possible tags with stream, much more complete than directly use the waterway=stream data from geofabrick or osmmx
    - dissolve the stream where name is the same
    - also add drain, ditch, culvert, river, for complete waterway lines as much as possible
    - output is [`data/stream_geometry/allblue.gpkg`](data/stream_geometry/allblue.gpkg)
- clean stream geometry
    - [`clean_stream_geometry.py`](scripts/clean_stream_geometry.py)    
    - from now on, the stream we use is the above [`allblue.gpkg`](data/stream_geometry/allblue.gpkg) and with its river removed
    - remove some of "drain" "ditch" when it is not connected to any other waterway or allblue polygon (remove unnecessary stream endings)
    - dissolve the ones with same name
    - merge to continuous lines
    - only keep the lines >=100m (remove the standalone short streams, since it is 100m, it is not considered in our analysis)
- Dresden watercourse data replaced (provided by Dresden-municipality-Anna)
    - [`clean_stream_geometry.py`](scripts/clean_stream_geometry.py)  
    - the output is [`data/stream_geometry/streamall.gpkg`](data/stream_geometry/streamall.gpkg)

# create segments
- below is for 100m
- 100m segment from WFD? cannot find the datasource for the 100m segmentation. 
    the WFD related values only vary when there is a sub-stream
- create 100m segments in [`create_stream_segments.py`](scripts/create_stream_segments.py). 
    - create points with 100m intervals
        - did not specifically identify the mouth of stream and start from there; sometimes the mouth is a waterbody, so there is always some missing, so I doubt the value of starting from mouth
    - create segments with snap to the point
- add original attribute
- only keep the ones with length between [99-101]m
    - when cutting the lines into 100m, the results show there are some minor variations, so 99 to 101 m is still acceptable
- remove overlaps
- output is [`data/stream_geometry/streamall_segment100.gpkg`](data/stream_geometry/streamall_segment100.gpkg), n=5745

- below is for 500m
- original idea is we select the smallest unit, as the basis. but now when we move to 500m, how about those 100m segments that cannot be included into any 500m segment?
    - actually to make the stream linstring connected, i also tried to connect the lines when there are waterbody in between of two segmented lines. but in many cases the waterboday is also not complete data available, then we just connect whenver there are gaps? not reliable either
- so now, the question is, when some segments are kept in 100m segment data, what should we do for those that cannot be included in any continuous 500m segment?
- first just try change the script from 100 to 500 (and 250)
    - create new field segment_id_500m
    - now the ones that are below 500(495?) are excluded
    - relate the 100mstream with the new 500m, where each 100m segment_id should correspond to the segment_id_500m
        - if there is no corresponding ones, just NULL
- or we just combine the segments when there are 500m continuous, (5 100m segements connected)


# calculate variable
## variable 1 impervious cover
- data needed: streamall_segment100, 100m buffer created for each stream segment, ESA worldcover 10m
- impervious ratio (built-up area / buffer area)
- [`calculate_impervious.py`](scripts/calculate_impervious.py)

## variable 2 canopy cover
- data needed: streamall_segment100, 50m buffer created for each stream segment, ESA worldcover 10m
- canopy cover (tree cover area / buffer area)
- [`calculate_impervious.py`](scripts/calculate_canopy.py)

## variable 3 green richness and shannon diversity index
- data needed: streamall_segment100, 150m buffer created for each stream segment, ESA worldcover 10m

## variable 4 land surface temperature
- data needed: streamall_segment100, 100m buffer created for each stream segment, Ermida GEE code 
    - Ermida, S.L., Soares, P., Mantas, V., Göttsche, F.-M., Trigo, I.F., 2020. 
        Google Earth Engine open-source code for Land Surface Temperature estimation from the Landsat series.
        Remote Sensing, 12 (9), 1471; https://doi.org/10.3390/rs12091471
    - mean of all LST (30m resolution) in each buffer

## variable 5 sinuosity
- data needed: streamall_segment300
- each 100m segment is checked to find a continuous sequence of three connected segments (i.e., previous, current, and next)
- segments at the beginning (with next two segments), middle (with both previous and next segments), or end (with previous two segments) are retained
- isolated segments or those without two-directional connections are excluded
- new 300m segments are created by merging the geometries of the three valid connected segments
- sinuosity is calculated as (length of merged geometry) / (straight-line distance from start to end point)


##  variable 6 crossability
- data needed: streamall_segment500, bridge+railway+road
- 


## variable 7 accessibility to POI
- data needed: streamall_segment500, OSM POI
- 

## variable 8 accessibility to public transport
- data needed: streamall_segment500, OSM bus/train/tram stop
- 

## variable 9 visibility
- data needed: streamall_segment500, CRisp
